PURCHASED THIS CAR BRAND NEW ABOUT A MONTH AGO. A LARGE FRACTURE CRACK APPEARED IN THE MIDDLE MY WINDSHIELD, ORIGINATING FROM THE BASE UNDERNEATH THE PLASTIC STRIPPING, EXTENDING UPWARD IN AN INVERTED L SHAPE. HAPPENED FOR NO APPARENT REASON; THE CAR WAS STATIONARY, PARKED INSIDE

THE CAR HAS STALLED 3 TIMES WHILE DRIVING.  AT A STOP SIGN, RED LIGHT AND AT A STOP SIGN.  THE INCIDENT AT THE TRAFFIC LIGHT CAUSED THE VEHICLE TO START ROLLING BACKWARDS AND ALMOST HIT INTO ANOTHER VEHICLE.  SUBARU CAN NOT FIND THE PROBLEM BUT ACKNOWLEDGES THE INCIDENTS OF STALL

ABOUT TWO WEEKS AGO WHEN THE FIRST FROST HIT I NOTICED THERE WAS A WINDSHIELD CRACK BUT IT LOOKED UNRELATED TO AN IMPACT CRACK AS IT CAME FROM THE SIDE OF THE WINDSHIELD AND EXTENDED TOWARDS THE CENTER. AFTER GETTING THE WINDSHIELD REPLACED ONE WEEK LATER WHILE SITTING IN MY CAR

narrative.headline: 2018 Subaru Impreza: exterior lighting tops the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 149 complaints with NHTSA. Exterior lighting is the most reported area at 43%, followed by visibility/wiper issues (22%). The engine complaints are fewer (7%) but the most serious by outcome, with the only reported fire and the only reported death. Worth a careful look on a test drive.

issues.sample: This recall was issued Aug 12 2022 and 3 months later still no parts available to fix the lighting issue? I reported this headlight issue at the first scheduled service that I thought the headlights were dim and did not shine far enough. Dealer service  sept said they are all lik
issues.title: Exterior Lighting
issues.blurb: The single biggest complaint: 64 reports (43%), linked to 1 crash. Owners describe dim or weak headlights, so check the lighting during a test drive.

issues.sample: PURCHASED THIS CAR BRAND NEW ABOUT A MONTH AGO. A LARGE FRACTURE CRACK APPEARED IN THE MIDDLE MY WINDSHIELD, ORIGINATING FROM THE BASE UNDERNEATH THE PLASTIC STRIPPING, EXTENDING UPWARD IN AN INVERTED L SHAPE. HAPPENED FOR NO APPARENT REASON; THE CAR WAS STATIONARY, PARKED INSIDE
issues.title: Visibility/Wiper
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 33 reports (22%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Owners often mention windshield cracks. Inspect the glass closely.

issues.sample: THE CAR HAS STALLED 3 TIMES WHILE DRIVING.  AT A STOP SIGN, RED LIGHT AND AT A STOP SIGN.  THE INCIDENT AT THE TRAFFIC LIGHT CAUSED THE VEHICLE TO START ROLLING BACKWARDS AND ALMOST HIT INTO ANOTHER VEHICLE.  SUBARU CAN NOT FIND THE PROBLEM BUT ACKNOWLEDGES THE INCIDENTS OF STALL
issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 12 reports (8%), tied to 1 crash and 1 injury. Some owners report stalling while driving, so watch for it on the road.

issues.sample: THE CAR HAS STALLED 3 TIMES WHILE DRIVING.  AT A STOP SIGN, RED LIGHT AND AT A STOP SIGN.  THE INCIDENT AT THE TRAFFIC LIGHT CAUSED THE VEHICLE TO START ROLLING BACKWARDS AND ALMOST HIT INTO ANOTHER VEHICLE.  SUBARU CAN NOT FIND THE PROBLEM BUT ACKNOWLEDGES THE INCIDENTS OF STALL
issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Only 11 reports (7%) but the most serious by outcome: the single reported fire, the single reported death, and 1 crash. Worth checking carefully.

issues.sample: ABOUT TWO WEEKS AGO WHEN THE FIRST FROST HIT I NOTICED THERE WAS A WINDSHIELD CRACK BUT IT LOOKED UNRELATED TO AN IMPACT CRACK AS IT CAME FROM THE SIDE OF THE WINDSHIELD AND EXTENDED TOWARDS THE CENTER. AFTER GETTING THE WINDSHIELD REPLACED ONE WEEK LATER WHILE SITTING IN MY CAR
issues.title: Structure
issues.blurb: The fewest complaints: 6 reports (4%), linked to 1 crash. A small but recurring area, with owners again noting windshield cracks.
